title: Struthanthus retusus
intro: Struthanthus retusus, commonly known as the "Struthanthus vine", is a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae, also known as a hemiparasitic epiphyte, which obtains its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. The health benefits of Struthanthus retusus include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Struthanthus retusus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Struthanthus retusus is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Struthanthus retusus is classified in the family Loranthaceae, commonly known as mistletoe family. This family belongs to the order Santalales, a group of flowering plants that are often known as sandalwood or mistletoe order. The order Santalales is part of the clade Asterids, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Struthanthus retusus is a species of mistletoe, a type of hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by connecting to the tissues of a host plant. The classification of Struthanthus retusus reflects its unique and specialized relationship with its host plant.
